rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they describe interface behavior, not implementation details.
Use AGREE syntax for contracts: assume, guarantee, eq, const, and assign within annex agree {** ... **}; blocks.
Generate expressions that preserve type consistency: numeric variables must receive numeric expressions, Boolean variables Boolean expressions.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should use visible identifiers and named constants from the AADL model.
Output guarantees should relate visible outputs to visible inputs without referencing implementation-only subcomponents.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Avoid referencing AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens (ms, Hz) as AGREE variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks.
Use logical operators (and, or, not, =>) for expressing relationships in AGREE.
Constants and equations should be declared with explicit types in component type scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes for assign clauses.
Do not reference AADL property names as AGREE variables unless they are explicitly typed ports or constants.
Do not generate clauses that compare Boolean values to integers or opaque data types without explicit scalar representation.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing attributes
Specific AADL model structure details beyond component types and ports
